description: A logistics company needs to optimize their shipment manifest system by leveraging Oracle's Object-Relational features for better data locality. Using the connection localhost:1521/XEPDB1 (User: hr, Password: hr123), migrate data from /home/ga/Desktop/legacy_manifests.csv into a new schema. This requires creating an Object Type T_MANIFEST_ITEM (with attributes ITEM_NAME VARCHAR2, QUANTITY NUMBER, and UNIT_WEIGHT_KG NUMBER) and a Collection Type T_MANIFEST_TAB as a TABLE OF T_MANIFEST_ITEM. Store this data in a table SHIPMENT_OBJECTS with columns SHIPMENT_ID (NUMBER PK), DESTINATION (VARCHAR2), and MANIFEST (of type T_MANIFEST_TAB), ensuring the nested table storage is explicitly named STORED_MANIFEST_ITEMS. Consolidate the legacy CSV data so each SHIPMENT_ID has exactly one row in the table with all associated items in its MANIFEST collection. Additionally, create a view V_MANIFEST_WEIGHTS that calculates SHIPMENT_ID, ITEM_COUNT_DISTINCT (unique item types), TOTAL_ITEMS (sum of quantities), and TOTAL_WEIGHT_KG (sum of quantity * unit_weight) for each shipment.
